title: Dichanthelium scoparium
taxonomy: Dichanthelium scoparium, commonly known as bearded rosette grass or bearded panic grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is classified under the genus Dichanthelium, which is a group of grasses native to North America. The genus Dichanthelium is itself a part of the subfamily Panicoideae, which comprises warm-season grasses. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as grasses. Dichanthelium scoparium is a perennial grass that typically grows in dry to moist areas, often in sandy or rocky soils.
uses: Dichanthelium scoparium, also known as bottlebrush grass, has been used in traditional medicine by various cultures.
